title: round them all screens, nanotech forms and shapes and creates whatever needed [b
content:
round them all screens, nanotech forms and shapes and creates whatever needed [be it furniture or food or open heart surgery], all connected to a virtual world through which to escape.

First person narrative would be derived from the printed output (written like code) found within the brain of each living and non living thing.

Third person narrative (our omniscient and omnipotent narrator, our muse, or moderator) would be derived from the data found on the hard drives of humanities' history (technology like how you function). This could be written in code or as plain text.

I haven't solved for a second person narrator.

And this is just the textual aspects of the work I'm creating. I will incorporate visual and audio elements as well which we need to solve.

Like technology evolves (and taking inspiration from finnegan's wake and a clockwork orange), the language evolved. I want to combine all the languages into a common tongue and heavily lean (to the absurd degree) into brevity and minimalism inspired by textspeak between humans (valuing brief incorrect spellings to save time), like rele for really.
context: From document: OK, can you provide me a detailed summary of all the ideas we went… — chunk 3 (chunk 3/18)
